The player moved down. The animation shows two objects:
1. "top-left (27,11) 4x18, 45 cells" - player + trail at new position
2. "top-left (32,11) 4x18, 45 cells" - player + trail at old position

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current positions after DOWN
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('R', 'b', 'N', 'M'):
        bnd = n['boundary']
        min_r = min(p[0] for p in bnd)
        min_c = min(p[1] for p in bnd)
        print(f"{n['color']}: top=({min_r},{min_c}), pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Check trail segments
trail_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'M' and n['pixels'] == 4]
print(f"Trail segments: {len(trail_nodes)}")
for n in trail_nodes:
    bnd = n['boundary']
    min_r = min(p[0] for p in bnd)
    min_c = min(p[1] for p in bnd)
    print(f"  Trail at ({min_r},{min_c})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
